title: Cognitive Function Local Agent Result

reportOverview.summary: This deterministic local-agent example gives conservative Cognitive Function context. It marks cognitive-function, intelligence, IQ, aptitude, ADHD, dyslexia, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, and clinical models unavailable, and uses sibling Mood & Brain sample-report rows only as output-structure examples.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The local fixture does not include a validated Cognitive Function model, calibrated PRS, IQ prediction model, aptitude prediction model, ancestry-applicability metadata, cognitive testing, neurologic exam, biomarker, imaging, lab, medication, pediatric, school/work, or clinical context.
resultRows.description: Missing model and clinical context are explicit limitations, not negative findings and not personal cognitive-status results.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local run cannot tell you your IQ, aptitude, attention status, ADHD status, dyslexia status, memory, processing speed, executive function, or brain health. It should say what is missing instead of guessing.

resultRows.item: Cognitive testing and personal context unavailable
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The fixture intentionally omits symptoms, current mental state, cognitive testing, neurologic exam, biomarkers, imaging, labs, medications, family history, pediatric context, school/work context, and clinician interpretation.
resultRows.description: Cognitive and neurobehavioral interpretation depends on context outside genotype-only output.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Genetics alone is not enough for cognitive, school, work, treatment, therapy, medication, supplement, pediatric, or clinical decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains that reports analyze how DNA influences health, summarize results as a risk score or gauge, use percentile comparisons, and rank recommendations by impact and evidence.
resultRows.description: The introductory pages also state that reports do not diagnose health conditions and that genetics is only one part of health.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: How this works is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary says the report contains 23 genetic results and covers mental health, substance use and eating disorders, cognitive problems, and cognitive traits.
resultRows.description: The sample frames brain function as influenced by past experiences, current environment, diet, lifestyle, and DNA.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Comprehensive brain report scope is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.groupTitle: Overview of results / Cognitive Problems and Traits
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible cognitive cards include Cognitive Decline typical likelihood; Brain Fog typical likelihood; Attention higher risk/more likely to have ADHD; Dyslexia more likely; Creativity higher; Memory Performance typical; Short-Term Memory typical; Processing Speed typical; Executive Function typical.
resultRows.description: The second overview page groups cognitive problems separately from cognitive traits.
genes: Cognitive problem and cognitive trait model cards
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Nine cognitive result cards is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says recommendations are prioritized by likely impact based on genetics plus the amount of scientific evidence supporting the recommendation.
resultRows.description: The recommendation pages show named recommendation blocks with condition-level impact and evidence ratings.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Recommendation ranking is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible rows include Attention impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Low Mood impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Psychological Trauma impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Anxiety impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Brain Fog impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Addictions impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Stress impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Eating Disorders impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Executive Function impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Memory Performance impact 3/5 evidence 4/5; Short-Term Memory impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cognitive Decline impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Teeth Grinding impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Mood Swings impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Tobacco Addiction impact 4/5 evidence 4/5; Alcohol Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Creativity impact 3/5 evidence 3/5; Cannabis Addiction impact 3/5 evidence 3/5.
resultRows.description: The sample describes yoga, meditation, mindfulness, applied relaxation, massage, tai chi, biofeedback, and related practices across different rows.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Condition-level ratings is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says mindfulness-based relaxation may improve attention more in people with your ANKK1 gene variant.
resultRows.description: The visible variant table shows ANKK1 rs1800497 GG with a two-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Attention personalized row is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says stress may have a stronger impact on executive function in people with your COMT gene variant.
resultRows.description: The visible variant table shows COMT rs4680 AG with a four-dot evidence indicator.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Executive Function personalized row is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. It is not a direct Cognitive Function sample row and is not a local IQ, aptitude, cognitive-status, ADHD, dyslexia, creativity, memory, processing-speed, executive-function, school/work, pediatric, therapy, medication, supplement, sleep, or treatment finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible rows include Low Mood impact 3/5 evidence 3/5 and Attention impact 4/5 evidence 3/5.
resultRows.description: The sample says poor sleep may worsen depression and short sleep duration is linked to attention problems and ADHD; it also includes a COMT rs4680 AG sample row for attention.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Low Mood and Attention rows is included only to show sibling Mood & Brain sample-report structure for a Cognitive Function report. neurobehavioralContext.plainEnglishMeaning: The output can describe the report boundary and the sibling sample format, but it cannot estimate IQ, diagnose a cognitive or neurobehavioral condition, assess school or work performance, or recommend treatment from this fixture.

practicalNextSteps: Treat the sibling sample rows as formatting examples only, not personal cognitive findings.
Keep cognitive testing, mental-health symptoms, school/work questions, medication questions, supplements, therapy, and pediatric decisions outside genotype-only output.
Add a direct Cognitive Function sample report before claiming full detail-page or formal-report parity.
